page: OVHcloud r2-30 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, running at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz on an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX). The hardware configuration includes 30.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 50 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate that the compute and memory performance of this instance falls into the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the server in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with small block cached reads in the bottom 25% and large block uncached reads and writes in the bottom 10%. Real-world application benchmarks, such as Redis SET operations and passmark database operations, also rank in the bottom 25%, while static web serving throughput falls in the bottom 10%.

The primary tradeoff of the r2-30 instance is its low compute and memory throughput relative to its high memory capacity. While it offers a dense memory allocation of 15.0 GB per core, the underlying Haswell-generation CPU architecture limits its processing speed. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intensive CPU calculations. It is suitable for lightweight database caching, small-scale memory-resident applications, and basic data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over execution speed.

Benchmark data indicates lower-tier performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests ranking in the bottom 10% to 25% of results. This hardware profile presents a tradeoff of high memory capacity against limited compute throughput. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands, such as lightweight database caching, small-scale memory-resident applications, and basic data processing tasks.
